attacking_style: Opportunistic on specialist terrain rather than pure sprinter; attacks or infiltrates moves on cobbles/technical finales (17km cobble breakaway win). In standard sprints, positioned late (2km mark) but fades in sustained drag races beyond 3km.
team_tactics: Bora-Hansgrohe employs timed late positioning via DRUCKER (2km deployment), avoiding premature commitment that burns rivals early. Less reliant on long leadout trains; can operate independently or through single pilot fish.
terrain_preference: Excels on cobbles and technical classics terrain where attrition creates smaller, selective groups. Struggles in sustained 3km+ drag sprints where top-end speed cannot be maintained. Prefers explosive finishes over power-endurance tests.
vulnerability: Lacks sustainable speed for extended drag sprints (3km+); fades when races become pure power contests. Physical battles against top-tier sprinters (SAGAN) expose limitations. Injury concerns affect consistency (crash impacts, post-2016 recovery timeline).
key_patterns: Demonstrates tactical intelligence through late positioning (2km) rather than raw sprinting power.
Performs best when attrition reduces bunch size; ninth-best against top tier in mass sprints.
Cobbled/technical terrain restores competitiveness; won from 17km cobble breakaway, resurgent form on specialist parcours.
Fades in physical battles against elite sprinters; beaten by SAGAN, VIVIANI despite good positioning.
Post-2016 injury trajectory shows fluctuating form; explosive speed retained but consistency variable across seasons.
form_trajectory: neutral: 2022 data shows diminished sustained speed and crash vulnerability, suggesting decline from 2018-2019 resurgence on specialist terrain
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 11
2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 13
